food_subgroup: Hot and cold mixed coffee-chocolate beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for Neslo Ice. Mapped to closest parent 'beverage food product' (FOODON_03301638).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: Neslo Ice is a blend of Nescafe (instant coffee) and Milo (chocolate malt drink) served over ice. FoodEx2 code AN000 (Coffee and coffee substitutes) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific blended beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Neslo Ice is approximately 250 ml (250 g), served in a tall glass or cup with ice.

nutrient_source.title: Milo (chocolate malt beverage, prepared) and Nescafe instant coffee with condensed milk — composite estimate based on IMR Malaysia MyFCD and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research Malaysia (IMR) MyFCD; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Neslo Ice is a blended drink of Nescafe instant coffee and Milo chocolate malt drink, typically prepared with condensed milk and served over ice. No single entry for 'Neslo Ice' exists in IMR MyFCD or USDA. Values are estimated from: (1) IMR MyFCD entry for 'Milo, prepared' and (2) USDA FoodData Central data for instant coffee with condensed milk. A standard 250 ml serving is assumed to contain approximately 60 ml condensed milk, 2 tsp Milo powder, 1 tsp Nescafe powder, and ice/water to volume. Per-100g values are derived from this composite.

gi_source.title: Milo (chocolate malt beverage) GI — analogous estimate; Condensed milk GI reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Neslo Ice. GI estimated at 55 (medium) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for Milo (chocolate malt drink, prepared with water/milk, GI ~55) and the known moderate GI of condensed milk (~60). Instant coffee contributes negligible glycemic load. The blended drink is dominated by Milo and condensed milk, supporting a composite GI estimate of 55. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (55 × 33.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 18.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and added sugar from condensed milk
hypertension.guidance: Neslo Ice contains approximately 95 mg sodium per 250 ml serving and a high sugar load from condensed milk; individuals with hypertension should limit intake and request reduced condensed milk or sugar-free variants.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content from condensed milk and Milo powder
type2_diabetes.guidance: A standard 250 ml serving of Neslo Ice delivers approximately 30 g of sugar, primarily from condensed milk, resulting in a glycemic load of ~18; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption and consider sugar-free or reduced-condensed-milk preparations.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and added sugar from condensed milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Neslo Ice contributes saturated fat (~2.5 g per serving) and high added sugar (~30 g per serving) from condensed milk, both of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes when consumed in excess; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency and portion size.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Neslo Ice is a popular Malaysian and Singaporean iced beverage made by blending Nescafe instant coffee with Milo, a chocolate malt drink powder, typically sweetened with condensed milk and evaporated milk, then poured over ice. The name 'Neslo' is a portmanteau of 'Nescafe' and 'Milo'. It is a staple at Malaysian kopitiams (traditional coffee shops) and hawker centres in Singapore, commonly ordered as a cold refreshment. The drink has a rich, sweet, chocolatey-coffee flavour with a creamy texture from the condensed milk. Nutritionally, Neslo Ice is energy-dense relative to its volume, with a high sugar content (~30 g per 250 ml serving) primarily from condensed milk, making it a moderate-to-high glycemic beverage (estimated GI ~55, GL ~18 per serving). It provides small amounts of calcium, iron, and B vitamins from the Milo component. Due to its high sugar and saturated fat content, it is not recommended for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. A typical serving size is 250 ml. It is halal-certified and vegetarian-friendly but not vegan due to dairy content.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Neslo Ice?
Is Neslo Ice high in sugar?
What is the difference between Neslo and Milo?
How much sugar is in Neslo Ice?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Neslo Ice as a composite beverage. Nutrient values are estimated from IMR Malaysia MyFCD (Milo prepared) and USDA FoodData Central (instant coffee with condensed milk). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using Milo as the closest match. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Neslo Ice is a blended beverage; no exact entry exists in IMR MyFCD or USDA FoodData Central. Per-100g values are composite estimates based on a standard preparation of 2 tsp Nescafe, 2 tsp Milo, 60 ml condensed milk, and ice/water to 250 ml. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for this composite. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for this composite. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for this composite. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for this composite. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for this composite. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for this composite. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as condensed milk and Milo powder contain negligible trans fat; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 as this beverage contains no significant vitamin C; confirmed zero per source data. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Grains and staples' to 'Beverages' as Neslo Ice is a liquid beverage. GI estimated at 55 (medium) using Milo (University of Sydney GI Database) as the closest analogous food.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is 'Neslo Ice', a precise canonical English name for this Malaysian/Singaporean blended coffee-chocolate iced beverage. All mandatory local-language names present: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g all present as numeric values. Source is IMR Malaysia MyFCD and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, both approved. nutrient_source documents the composite estimation basis.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.2), sodium_mg (38.0), saturated_fat_g (1.0), sugar_g (12.0) all present as numeric values and plausible for a condensed-milk-based beverage.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index=55 (numeric, >0), gi_category='medium', glycemic_load_per_serving=18.2, gi_source references University of Sydney GI Database with analogous_estimate basis documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn mapped to FOODON_03301638 (beverage food product, closest_parent). FoodEx2 mapped to AN000 (Coffee and coffee substitutes, closest_parent). SNOMED CT is null with gap note. No codes invented. All match_type values use only allowed values: exact, closest_parent, or none.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains what the food is, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate), Type 2 Diabetes (high), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is 'Type 2 Diabetes'.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list is comprehensive with 14 entries covering English variants, Malay variants, Chinese script, Tamil script, Hindi script, and romanized forms. aliases_by_language is fully consistent with flat aliases list — all entries in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
